user.title: Sensor Mapping
user.description: Map your Home Assistant sensors to Solar Power Control. You must provide at least one of: Grid Export, Combined Import/Export, or Load Power.

data_description.pv_power: Sensor that reports your current PV production (W or kW — automatically converted).
data_description.grid_export: Sensor that reports power exported to the grid (W or kW — automatically converted).
data_description.import_export_power: Combined sensor where positive = export, negative = import (W or kW — automatically converted). Use this instead of Grid Export if your inverter provides a single combined sensor.
data_description.load_power: Sensor that reports your total household load (W or kW — automatically converted). When provided, excess is calculated as PV production minus load.
data_description.grid_voltage: Your local grid voltage (typically 230 V in Europe, 120 V in North America). Used for dynamic current calculations.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure update interval and control behaviour.

data_description.off_threshold: How far into negative excess (W) the controller tolerates before starting to shed appliances. Default: -50 W.
data_description.controller_interval: How often the controller reads sensors and adjusts appliances.
data_description.enable_preemption: When enabled, the optimizer can shed lower-priority appliances to free up power for higher-priority ones.

error.no_grid_sensor: You must configure at least one of: Grid Export sensor, Combined Import/Export sensor, or Load Power sensor.

abort.already_configured: Solar Power Control is already configured.

appliance.title: Appliance
appliance.description: Add an appliance to be controlled based on surplus solar power.

user.title: Add Appliance
user.description: Configure the basic information and power profile for this appliance.

data_description.appliance_name: A friendly name for this appliance.
data_description.appliance_entity: The switch, light, climate or other controllable entity.
data_description.appliance_priority: Lower number = higher priority. Appliances with higher priority receive surplus power first.
data_description.nominal_power: The rated power consumption of this appliance in watts.
data_description.actual_power_entity: Optional sensor that reports actual power draw (W or kW — automatically converted). Used for more accurate analytics.
data_description.phases: Number of electrical phases this appliance uses. Relevant for dynamic current calculations.

current.title: Dynamic Current Settings
current.description: Configure dynamic current control for variable-power appliances such as EV chargers.

data_description.dynamic_current: Enable to control this appliance by adjusting its charging current rather than simple on/off.
data_description.current_entity: Number entity used to set the charging current (e.g., input_number or number from your charger integration).
data_description.min_current: Minimum operating current in amperes. The appliance will turn off rather than drop below this level.
data_description.current_step: Resolution of current adjustments. Use 0.1 A for fine-grained control or 1.0 A for whole-amp steps.
data_description.ev_soc_entity: Sensor that reports the EV battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.ev_connected_entity: Binary sensor that indicates whether the EV is plugged in. Charging stops when disconnected.
data_description.ev_target_soc: Stop charging when the EV reaches this state of charge.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: Configure switch interval, activation threshold, and appliance dependencies.

data_description.switch_interval: Minimum time in seconds between switching this appliance on and off. Prevents rapid cycling.
data_description.averaging_window: Custom time window in seconds over which excess power is averaged before making decisions. Leave empty to use the default rolling window.
data_description.on_only: When enabled, the integration will turn this appliance on when surplus is available but will never turn it off automatically.
data_description.protect_from_preemption: When enabled, this appliance will not be shed to make room for a higher-priority appliance.
data_description.on_threshold: Additional surplus power (watts) required above the appliance's rated consumption before activation. Leave empty for the default (200 W).
data_description.completion_power_threshold: When actual power drops below this threshold the appliance is considered done and runtime tracking pauses. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.requires_appliance: Select an appliance that must already be running before this one can start. For example, a pool heat pump that requires the circulation pump.
data_description.helper_only: When enabled, this appliance only starts when another appliance lists it as its required dependency. It never starts on its own.

reconfigure_basic.title: Edit Appliance
reconfigure_basic.description: Update the basic information and power profile for this appliance.

data_description.appliance_name: A friendly name for this appliance.
data_description.appliance_entity: The switch, light, climate or other controllable entity.
data_description.appliance_priority: Lower number = higher priority. Appliances with higher priority receive surplus power first.
data_description.nominal_power: The rated power consumption of this appliance in watts.
data_description.actual_power_entity: Optional sensor that reports actual power draw (W or kW — automatically converted).
data_description.phases: Number of electrical phases this appliance uses.

data_description.dynamic_current: Enable to control this appliance by adjusting its charging current rather than simple on/off.
data_description.current_entity: Number entity used to set the charging current.
data_description.ev_soc_entity: Sensor that reports the EV battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.ev_connected_entity: Binary sensor that indicates whether the EV is plugged in.
data_description.ev_target_soc: Stop charging when the EV reaches this state of charge.

reconfigure_constraints.title: Edit Constraints
reconfigure_constraints.description: Update switch interval, activation threshold, and appliance dependencies.

data_description.switch_interval: Minimum time in seconds between switching this appliance on and off.
data_description.averaging_window: Custom time window in seconds over which excess power is averaged. Leave empty to use the default.
data_description.on_only: When enabled, the integration will turn this appliance on but never off automatically.
data_description.protect_from_preemption: When enabled, this appliance will not be shed to make room for a higher-priority appliance.
data_description.on_threshold: Additional surplus power required above rated consumption before activation. Leave empty for the default (200 W).
data_description.completion_power_threshold: When actual power drops below this threshold the appliance is considered done. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.requires_appliance: Select an appliance that must already be running before this one can start.
data_description.helper_only: When enabled, this appliance only starts when another appliance lists it as its required dependency.

error.missing_entity: An appliance entity must be selected.
error.invalid_power: Nominal power must be greater than 0.
error.missing_current_entity: A current control entity is required when dynamic current is enabled.
error.invalid_current_range: Minimum current must be less than maximum current.
error.circular_dependency: Circular dependency: the selected appliance already depends on this one.
error.helper_only_with_requires: An appliance cannot be both a helper and have its own dependency. Either clear 'Requires Appliance' or uncheck 'Run Only as Helper'.

abort.reconfigure_not_supported: Reconfigure is not supported on this Home Assistant version. Please delete and re-add the appliance.

user.description: Update your sensor mappings and grid voltage.

data_description.grid_export: Sensor that reports power exported to the grid (W or kW — automatically converted).

data_description.load_power: Sensor that reports your total household load (W or kW — automatically converted).
data_description.grid_voltage: Your local grid voltage. Used for dynamic current calculations.

data_description.controller_interval: How often the controller reads sensors and adjusts appliances.
data_description.enable_preemption: When enabled, the optimizer can shed lower-priority appliances to free up power for higher-priority ones.
